modal.unlink_google_message: This unlinks local Google Calendar access for {label}. Synced Google calendar schemes remain, but they will show as offline until you sign in locally again.
onboarding.step.calendar.body: Your calendar holds events, assignments, and reminders. Click to add a reminder, shift-click for an assignment, or drag to block out an event.
onboarding.step.calendar.title: Calendar
onboarding.step.daily.body: Daily is a special, default scheme. Write an optimistic task list each day and check off the ones you complete.
onboarding.step.daily.title: Daily
onboarding.step.schemes.body: Schemes are editable outlines for projects, notes, and plans. Add start and end times to any line to turn it into a calendar item.
onboarding.step.schemes.title: Schemes
onboarding.step.upcoming.body: Upcoming gathers nearby events, assignments, and reminders. You can mark tasks complete right from here.
onboarding.step.upcoming.title: Upcoming
onboarding.step.welcome.body: KnotQ is a single app for calendar events, reminders, assignments, and general notes. It aims to be simple yet functional.
onboarding.step.welcome.title: Welcome to KnotQ
onboarding.sync_prompt.body: Sync is $3.99 a month and lets you share your workspace across devices. Local-only is fully free.

web.delete_account.contact_fallback_a: If you can no longer sign in, or you would prefer that we handle it for you, email
web.delete_account.contact_fallback_b: from the address associated with your account and ask us to delete your account. Do not send your password, OAuth tokens, or other secrets by email. We may ask you to verify ownership of the account before deleting it.
web.delete_account.deleted_item_account_: Your account record, including your email address and password hash.
web.delete_account.deleted_item_auth: Authentication sessions, refresh tokens, sign-in challenges, and authorization codes.
web.delete_account.deleted_item_billing: Your subscription and billing-event records held by KnotQ.
web.delete_account.deleted_item_docs_crd: Synced workspace documents and CRDT sync state.
web.delete_account.deleted_item_media: Media you synced (such as images embedded in items), including the stored files.
web.delete_account.deleted_item_workspac: Your synced workspace: schemes, folders, tasks, calendar entries, and any imported Google Calendar entries that were part of your synced workspace.
web.delete_account.grace_period_p1_a: Deletion is not immediate. When you request deletion, your account is scheduled for permanent removal after a
web.delete_account.grace_period_p1_b: , and all of your sign-in sessions are revoked right away. During those 14 days your email address cannot be used to create a new account.
web.delete_account.grace_period_p2: If you change your mind, simply sign back in with your email and password during the grace period and the scheduled deletion is automatically cancelled. After the 14 days elapse, deletion is permanent and cannot be undone.
web.delete_account.heading: Delete your account
web.delete_account.how_to_request_intro: You can delete your account yourself, from inside the app or from this website:
web.delete_account.intro_p1_a: This page explains how to request deletion of your
web.delete_account.intro_p1_b: account and the data associated with it. A KnotQ account only exists if you chose to enable optional KnotQ Sync. If you never created a KnotQ account, your KnotQ data is stored only on your own device and there is no account to delete — see
web.delete_account.kept_backups_logs_tra: may contain residual copies for a short period before they age out on their normal rotation.
web.delete_account.kept_payment_records_: held by our payment provider, Lemon Squeezy, are retained under their own terms and tax/accounting requirements, separate from KnotQ.
web.delete_account.kept_sessions_billing: may be retained for up to
web.delete_account.local_only_body: If you never enabled KnotQ Sync, your workspace lives only on your device and is never sent to KnotQ's servers. To remove that data, delete the KnotQ app's local data or uninstall the app. Disconnecting Google Calendar inside KnotQ removes the local Google credentials and stops further access; previously imported events remain in your local workspace until you remove those calendar sources or clear the app's data.
web.delete_account.questions_a: For anything about account deletion, data retention, or KnotQ support, email
web.delete_account.step1_a: Sign in to your KnotQ account, either in the KnotQ app or on the
web.delete_account.step2_a: Open the account screen and choose
web.delete_account.step3: Confirm by typing your account email address and your current password.
web.delete_account.step4: Confirm the deletion. Your account is then scheduled for deletion and you are signed out on all devices.
web.delete_account.store_subscription_b: automatically cancel a subscription you purchased through the Google Play Store or Apple App Store. If you subscribed through an app store, cancel the subscription directly in that store's subscription settings to stop future charges.
web.delete_account.subtitle: How to delete your KnotQ account and what happens to your data.
web.delete_account.title: Delete Your Account — KnotQ
web.delete_account.what_gets_deleted_int: After the grace period, the following data is permanently removed from KnotQ's systems:
web.delete_account.what_is_kept_heading: What is kept, and for how long
web.delete_account.what_is_kept_intro: To operate the service securely and meet accounting obligations, a limited set of records may persist briefly after deletion:
web.guide.calendar.body: The calendar is a week-view grid that pulls events from all your schemes, color-coded by scheme. Click any event to jump to its source line in the editor. The upcoming panel on the left shows what's due next and is visible on every page.
web.guide.calendar.image_alt: KnotQ weekly calendar with events, assignments, and reminders from multiple schemes.
web.guide.calendar.title: Calendar
web.guide.daily.image_alt: KnotQ Daily Queue showing today's task list with carried-over items from yesterday.
web.guide.daily.p1_post: gives you a fresh page every day. Incomplete items optionally carry over from the previous day, whereas completed tasks stay behind.
web.guide.daily.p2_post: I want to finish. It's important these are specific items so that they are actionable. Throughought the day, I go through and check them off as I complete each one.
web.guide.daily.p2_pre: My workflow: at the start of the day (or the end of yesterday), I write an
web.guide.daily.p2_strong: optimistic list of concrete, small tasks
web.guide.daily.p3_post: about what to do next. When you finish a task the next one is right there. The list being optimistic also means it almost always lasts me the entire day.
web.guide.daily.p3_strong: you never have to make a decision
web.guide.daily.p4: Before KnotQ, I found myself in the paradoxical state of having so many things I need to get done yet not doing anything because everything was such large task. Breaking these into actionable tasks daily helped me a lot.
web.guide.daily.title: Daily Queue
web.guide.google_calendar.body: KnotQ can display your Google Calendar events alongside your schemes (read-only for now). You can create a new Google Calendar scheme by pressing new in the left sidebar.
web.guide.google_calendar.title: Google Calendar
web.guide.intro: KnotQ is a free productivity app that integrates notes with a calendar for general purpose organization. Workspaces are local-first and owned by you, with optional account sync.
web.guide.meta_description: Learn how to use KnotQ: schemes, scheduling, the Daily Queue, Google Calendar import, search, and keyboard shortcuts.
web.guide.scheduling.image_alt: KnotQ date picker popover open on a line in the scheme editor.
web.guide.scheduling.intro: Any line can be scheduled by giving it a start time, end time, or both:
web.guide.scheduling.p_mid1: to set a start date and
web.guide.scheduling.p_mid2: to set an end date. Both open a popover inline so you can pick a time without leaving the document. Use
web.guide.scheduling.p_post: to add a repeat rule. You can also create items directly from the calendar: click a time slot for a reminder, shift+click for an assignment, or drag for an event.
web.guide.scheduling.title: Scheduling
web.guide.schemes.image_alt: KnotQ scheme editor showing a document with headings, checkboxes, and nested items.
web.guide.schemes.p1_post: at the bottom of the sidebar or with
web.guide.schemes.p2_post: to indent and create nested structure.
web.guide.schemes.p2_pre: Lines can be headings, checkmarks, bullets, numbered steps, or plain notes. Use
web.guide.schemes.title: Schemes
web.guide.search.p_post: to search across all schemes and calendar items.
web.guide.search.title: Search
web.guide.shortcuts.close_search_or_popo: Close search, cancel rename/delete, stop calendar drag, or close the active popover
web.guide.shortcuts.move_to_start_or_end: Move to start or end of document
Move to start or end of line
web.guide.shortcuts.open_selected_search: Open the selected search result, confirm rename, or close an event popup
web.guide.shortcuts.select_to_start_or_e: Select to start or end of document
Select to start or end of line
web.guide.shortcuts.show_character_palet: Show the character palette on macOS
web.guide.shortcuts.title: Keyboard shortcuts
web.guide.title: Guide
web.home.download.app_store_aria: KnotQ for iOS is coming soon
web.home.download.body: Free on Android, macOS, Windows, and Linux. iOS and sync are coming soon.
web.home.download.linux_body: Install from the latest GitHub release.
web.home.download.macos_body: Download the DMG for your Mac.
web.home.download.mobile_note: KnotQ for iOS is coming soon.
web.home.download.title: Get KnotQ
web.home.faq.q1_answer: Yes. The KnotQ desktop app is free and works fully offline, with no account required. KnotQ Sync is coming soon.
web.home.faq.q2_answer: Completely. KnotQ is local-first: your schemes, tasks, and calendar live in plain files on your device and work without an internet connection. Sync is coming soon and will be optional.
web.home.faq.q3_answer: Not yet. KnotQ Sync is coming soon; the current release is local-first and stores your workspace on your device.
web.home.faq.q4_answer: A local-first planner stores your data on your own device first, rather than on someone else’s server. KnotQ keeps your notes, tasks, deadlines, and calendar as plain files you own, so the app stays fast, private, and available offline.
web.home.faq.q5_answer: KnotQ is built on schemes — markdown-style documents. Add a start date, an end date, or both to any line and it becomes a calendar item: an event, an assignment, or a reminder. Everything you schedule shows up in one week view.
web.home.faq.q5_summary: How does KnotQ turn notes into a calendar?
web.home.faq.q6_answer: Yes. KnotQ can import your Google Calendar read-only and merge it into the same week view as your plans, so you see meetings, classes, and self-scheduled tasks together.
web.home.faq.q6_summary: Can I import my Google Calendar?
web.home.faq.q7_answer: KnotQ runs on Android, macOS (Apple Silicon and Intel), Windows, and Linux. iOS and cross-device sync are coming soon.
web.home.faq.title: Common questions about KnotQ
web.home.features.daily.body: The Daily Queue is today’s short list. Whatever you don’t finish rolls into tomorrow; what you finish stays behind.
web.home.features.daily.image_alt: KnotQ Daily Queue showing today's tasks, notes, and carried-over items from yesterday.
web.home.features.daily.title: Work from today
web.home.features.scheduling.body: Add a start, an end, or both to a line and it lands on your calendar — as an event, assignment, or reminder.
web.home.features.scheduling.image_alt: KnotQ date picker popover open on a line in the scheme editor.
web.home.features.scheduling.title: Any line becomes a calendar item
web.home.features.schemes.body: KnotQ is a planning workspace built on schemes — structured documents with headings, checkboxes, and nested items. Write a project the way you think about it.
web.home.features.schemes.image_alt: KnotQ scheme editor showing a product roadmap document with tasks, checkboxes, and inline date pickers.
web.home.features.schemes.title: Your plans live as documents
web.home.hero.image_alt: KnotQ calendar showing a packed week with classes, meetings, and due assignments.
web.home.hero.lead: KnotQ turns markdown documents into a live schedule.
web.home.hero.meta: Free · Android, macOS, Windows, Linux · iOS and sync coming soon
web.home.hero.title: Reminders, Assignments, Events, and Plans in one workspace
web.home.meta_description: KnotQ is a local-first desktop planning workspace where structured notes become tasks, deadlines, reminders, and calendar events.
web.home.meta_title: KnotQ — Reminders, assignments, events, and plans in one workspace
web.home.pitch.tile1_body: Plans from every scheme, plus your Google Calendar, in a single week view.
web.home.pitch.tile2_body: Drop a reminder, due date, or event onto any line as you write — no separate forms or fields.
web.home.pitch.tile3_body: Local-first plain files that work fully offline. No account required.
web.home.pitch.tile4_body: Work from a clean Daily Queue — unfinished tasks carry forward so nothing slips through.
web.home.pitch.title: Planning without splitting the plan apart
web.home.sync.body: KnotQ is free and works fully offline — no account needed. KnotQ Sync is coming soon for keeping your workspace and notifications in step across devices.
web.home.sync.point2: Schemes, calendar, and media will stay in step
web.home.sync.point3: Always optional — your data stays on your device too
web.home.sync.title: Start local. Sync when you’re ready.
